TextENGNew: The hours during which phone calls could be put through to hospital patients were strictly fixed.
...Which meant no more calls from him today.
After she'd teased him yesterday about being lonely, he might have agonized over whether to call before ultimately deciding to hold back.
changeReason: The new translation includes the detail that he 'decided to hold back' (我慢した), which the old translation omits, changing the implication from mere hesitation to active restraint.

TextENGNew: That was a far more convincing explanation than imagining he was simply too busy.
...Because that's the kind of person he was.@kYukie murmured this to herself and smiled.
The announcement that visiting hours were over played over the hospital intercom along with some music.
She exchanged greetings with the family visiting the patient in the next bed -- a familiar sight by now.@kTheir small child was wearing a radiant smile, no doubt eager for the mother's discharge.
The child's mother lay in the bed beside mine, carrying what would be the child's new brother or sister.
They were probably ready to burst with the expectations, dreams, and worries of having a new sibling.
The joys of a growing family.
...Bathed in those warm feelings, I stroked my own swelling belly.
I had talked with him about how many children we wanted to have.
We talked about how if we had three, it would certainly be lively.
...But there was an inescapable reality I could not look away from: the worry of whether my body could endure that many births.
"But no mother would shy away from childbirth just because of that worry..."
Yukie, smiling as she said that to herself, gently stroked her stomach.
The Metropolitan Public Safety Division.
The place where his sense of justice had ultimately led him.
...He was, in truth, an extraordinarily gentle and easily wounded person.
He rarely shared the details, but... I felt his current work was not suited to him.
But... as long as he said he was going to try his best, I would watch over him warmly.
"Your father, you see... is a very hard worker. Coochie coochie coo...☆"
Yukie seemed to be enjoying herself as she talked to her own belly.
...At that moment, Yukie suddenly became concerned about something, and looked out the window.
It was evening.
changeReason: The old translation 'It used to be this time...' is inaccurate; the Japanese (刻限は夕方) simply states 'It was evening,' which the new translation correctly renders.

TextENGNew: Long ago.@kShe remembered that when she was small, at her grandmother's country home, the air at this hour would be filled with the chorus of evening cicadas.
This was the middle of Tokyo.
Unlike in the countryside, you couldn't hear the song of the higurashi.
...Yet for some reason... at that moment, Yukie felt she wanted to hear the song of the higurashi.
